title: dianthus caesius subsp. montanus
intro: Dianthus caesius subsp. montanus, also known as the alpine pink, is a subspecies of the Dianthus caesius species, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The medicinal properties of Dianthus caesius subsp. montanus lie in its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and colds. In herbal preparations, Dianthus caesius subsp. montanus can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
